page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at 2.3 GHz, the server allocates 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, resulting in 2 threads per core. It features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core execution capabilities, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates placing in the top tier. However, multi-threaded scaling presents a bottleneck; multi-core stress-ng, PassMark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression benchmarks fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is also split, with small-block cached read bandwidth performing in the middle 50% at 344.38 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read bandwidth and overall PassMark memory scores drop to the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highcpu-4 offers high single-thread compute density relative to its resource footprint, making it efficient for workloads that do not heavily saturate memory bandwidth or require massive multi-core scaling. The primary tradeoff is the low memory allocation of 2.0 GB per core and poor uncached memory performance, which limits its utility for memory-intensive applications.

This instance is suited for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight web serving, and basic text processing. It is less suitable for large-scale software compilation, database operations, or medium-to-large language model inference, where memory bandwidth and multi-core performance constraints become prominent.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server configured with 4 vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 260 MB L3 cache, and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmarks indicate exceptional single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression tests ranking in the top 10%. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth for large blocks are weaker, falling into the bottom 25%. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for single-thread-heavy tasks but presents tradeoffs in memory-intensive scenarios. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and single-threaded workloads, rather than database operations or multi-threaded software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, delivering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, built for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized server delivering top-tier single-core performance and advanced matrix extensions for specialized single-threaded processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 4 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM
Top-tier single-core and single-threaded compression performance
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and AMX
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Weaker multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight web serving and text processing
